title: Island of Bones
author: Steven Saint
status_detail: Books 1–6 drafted; Book 7 in progress; Books 8–9 in beat-sheet form.
elevator_pitch: Five centuries ago the official story of the New World was carefully written over an earlier arrival. A scattered visual cipher — the Codec — was hidden across the Caribbean to prove it, aimed at a vault on Cayo Hueso, the island of bones. The modern team that reassembles the Codec is not the first to try. The line that hid it has been waiting a thousand years for them to read it.
tagline: Now they'll never know we were here first.
voice: Dan Brown machinery, Patterson velocity, Holmesian deduction; King-edge dread closer to Lisey's Story than to It.
register: Close-third multi-POV — Iris Lettsome (lead), Kwame Cudjoe (partner), Rafael Ribero (antagonist); period-thread interludes across six eras.
structure: Three core volumes — The Island of Bones · The River Turn · The Vault — followed by a prequel (Book 4), a southern-branch sequel (Book 5), an origin trilogy (Books 6–8), and a futuristic capstone (Book 9).
settings_hero: Virgin Islands — St. John, St. Thomas, Norman Island (BVI), Salt River (St. Croix)
Mississippi corridor — Vicksburg and the lower Gulf
Mesoamerica, the Bahamas, and the deep-past Vinland window
